reasoning: The resolution period covers December 30, 2024, to December 31, 2025. Bitcoin prices on December 30 and 31, 2024, were solidly around $93,000 to $94,000. Throughout 2025, Bitcoin experienced an upward trend, hitting an all-time high over $126,000 and mostly oscillating between $70,000 and $100,000+. Major crypto reports explicitly state the lowest points in 2025 were around $70,000–$80,000. A highly publicized flash crash occurred on Binance on Christmas Day 2025, where the price dropped to $24,111; however, multiple financial reports emphasize that this flash crash was entirely isolated to the BTC/USD1 trading pair (a pair for a new stablecoin) and did not occur on the BTC/USDT pair or other major markets. Because the resolution criteria explicitly mandate the use of the Binance BTCUSDT pair and specifically exclude other pairs, the flash crash does not trigger a "Yes" resolution. Since there is no record of the BTCUSDT pair dropping to $50,000 or below at any point during the resolution period, the probability of a "Yes" resolution is roughly 2%, allowing only a marginal chance of an unreported 1-minute flash wick.
